featuredTiles.headline: Three top-7 rookies have zero autos
featuredTiles.stat: VJ Edgecombe, Tre Johnson III, Jeremiah Fears
featuredTiles.caption: The #3, #6, and #7 picks in the 2025 NBA Draft have base cards and inserts but not a single autograph. Two more first-rounders (Carter Bryant #14, Hugo Gonzalez #28) are also auto-less — but the top-7 cluster is the Panini-exclusive fingerprint.

featuredTiles.headline: The Flagg brothers share a 1st Bowman
featuredTiles.caption: Cooper Flagg anchors the NBA base set at #1. Younger brother Ace Flagg lands on BPP-91 at Maine. Sibling set chase is real.

featuredTiles.headline: Ray Allen's Young Kings card is a Supersonic
featuredTiles.stat: A team that hasn't existed since 2008
featuredTiles.caption: YK-25 features Ray Allen in Seattle green — the franchise Oklahoma City took over. Topps deliberately chose the signature era.

featuredTiles.headline: Every card has a 1/1 parallel
featuredTiles.caption: Every Base has Platinum Border /1. Every Paper Prospect has Platinum Border /1. Every Chrome Prospect has SuperFractor /1. Holy Grail ceiling is universal.

featuredTiles.headline: Three teams tied for most cards
featuredTiles.caption: The deepest allocations in the product: Brooklyn's loaded rookie class, Kentucky's men-plus-women mix, UConn's Sarah Strong-led depth.

featuredTiles.headline: Cameron Boozer: zero cards, full marketing
featuredTiles.caption: Topps' education sheet features Boozer as a top 2026 prospect. The actual checklist doesn't include him. Twin brother Cayden is also missing.

allTiles.headline: Three top-7 rookies have zero autos
allTiles.stat: VJ Edgecombe, Tre Johnson III, Jeremiah Fears
allTiles.caption: The #3, #6, and #7 picks in the 2025 NBA Draft have base cards and inserts but not a single autograph. Two more first-rounders (Carter Bryant #14, Hugo Gonzalez #28) are also auto-less — but the top-7 cluster is the Panini-exclusive fingerprint.

allTiles.headline: The Flagg brothers share a 1st Bowman
allTiles.caption: Cooper Flagg anchors the NBA base set at #1. Younger brother Ace Flagg lands on BPP-91 at Maine. Sibling set chase is real.

allTiles.headline: Ray Allen's Young Kings card is a Supersonic
allTiles.stat: A team that hasn't existed since 2008
allTiles.caption: YK-25 features Ray Allen in Seattle green — the franchise Oklahoma City took over. Topps deliberately chose the signature era.

allTiles.headline: Every card has a 1/1 parallel
allTiles.caption: Every Base has Platinum Border /1. Every Paper Prospect has Platinum Border /1. Every Chrome Prospect has SuperFractor /1. Holy Grail ceiling is universal.

allTiles.headline: Three teams tied for most cards
allTiles.caption: The deepest allocations in the product: Brooklyn's loaded rookie class, Kentucky's men-plus-women mix, UConn's Sarah Strong-led depth.

allTiles.headline: Cameron Boozer: zero cards, full marketing
allTiles.caption: Topps' education sheet features Boozer as a top 2026 prospect. The actual checklist doesn't include him. Twin brother Cayden is also missing.

allTiles.headline: Four insert sets. Same 25 players. Same odds.
allTiles.stat: Talent Tracker · Gen Next · VIP · Bowman Verified
allTiles.caption: Topps shipped the same 25-player NCAA pool across four structurally identical inserts with different wrapper art. Talent Tracker is the flagship; the rest are filler.

allTiles.headline: Shaq is in this product three different ways
allTiles.caption: One player, three eras, three cards. Modern star, college throwback, college throwback autograph. Only Shaq in the Retrofractor auto since Bill Russell is deceased.

allTiles.headline: 30% of prospects are women
allTiles.caption: First serious women's basketball representation in a Bowman NBA product. Juju Watkins, Madison Booker, and Sarah Strong headline.

allTiles.headline: Hannah Hidalgo is the glaring WCBB absence
allTiles.caption: The most obvious omission in the women's lineup. One of the most-demanded NIL names in college basketball, not in the product.

allTiles.headline: Easiest auto pull in the product: 1:72
allTiles.caption: Wemby, Cade, Edey, Risacher sign here. Most accessible path to an NBA star auto — though the individual print runs are wide.

allTiles.headline: The entire Mojo rainbow is Mega-only
allTiles.caption: If you want Mojo, you buy Mega. Blue Mojo /150 down to Rose Gold Mojo /1 — the rarest single parallel in the product, and only in one format.

allTiles.headline: Sapphire parallels have no per-pack odds
allTiles.caption: Topps doesn't publish odds for Sapphire because every pack in a Sapphire Box is a Sapphire. Format economics replace pack economics.

allTiles.headline: 1,500+ unique 1/1 cards in the base checklist alone
allTiles.caption: Platinum Border, Chrome SuperFractor, Padparadscha Sapphire, Black Mojo, Rose Gold Mojo — each base, paper prospect, and chrome prospect card has multiple /1 parallels. Insert /1s push the total well above 2,000. Scarcity at scale.

allTiles.headline: Retrofractor is a 2-card legend variation
allTiles.caption: The sneaky best case hit. College-era photography of two HOFers who never had a 1st Bowman before. Russell's imagery alone is a rarity.

allTiles.headline: Dallas has 5 cards. One is Flagg.
allTiles.caption: The smallest NBA team allocation pairs 4 veterans with the face of the draft. Per-card, Dallas is the most Flagg-dependent team in the product.

allTiles.headline: Duke's Boozer paradox
allTiles.stat: Top 2026 prospects · zero Duke Boozer cards
allTiles.caption: Cameron and Cayden Boozer both play for Duke. Both are top 2026 NBA Draft projections. Neither is in the product. The Boozer no-show is Duke's biggest storyline.

allTiles.headline: First NBA Bowman in 17 years
allTiles.caption: Bowman Basketball's NBA identity has been dormant since 2008-09. The Fanatics/Topps NBA exclusive reopens the door — this is the first product through it.

allTiles.headline: Anime is projected to outperform every other case hit
allTiles.stat: Carryover from Bowman Baseball's biggest chase
allTiles.caption: Anime has been Topps' most consistently over-performing case hit in baseball. Basketball demand projected to match or exceed. Flagg and Dybantsa lead the 25-card NBA run.

allTiles.headline: Kentucky's 10 cards span both genders
allTiles.caption: Jasper Johnson anchors the men. Kaelyn Carroll leads the women. Kentucky is the deepest dual-gender NCAA allocation in the product.

allTiles.headline: UConn's 10 cards are anchored by Sarah Strong
allTiles.caption: UConn shows up heavier on the women's side than most programs. Strong is the Juju Watkins of New England — only with more room to grow.

caseHitProbability.description: Approximate case-level case-hit probability across all 11 case-hit inserts and variations. Assumes standard 12-box Hobby case.
caseHitProbability.pullRate: ~1-2 case hits per Hobby case on average
caseHitProbability.notes: Specific case hits vary: Anime NBA and Spotlights NBA are tightest (1:1,663-1:1,733 Hobby) while Retrofractor Variation and GPK NBA are deeper (1:13,050). Multi-case orders recommended for specific chases.
caseHitProbability.source: Calculated from individual case-hit odds in Topps odds PDF

featuredVisualizations.title: The Panini Shadow
featuredVisualizations.subtitle: 2025 NBA Draft picks 1-30 and their Bowman auto exposure
featuredVisualizations.caption: Five first-round picks have zero autograph cards in this product: VJ Edgecombe (#3), Tre Johnson III (#6), Jeremiah Fears (#7), Carter Bryant (#14), and Hugo Gonzalez (#28). The three top-7 absences are the headline — top-pick scarcity is likely Panini-exclusive auto contracts shaping a Topps release.

featuredVisualizations.title: Young Kings: Four Decades on One Insert
featuredVisualizations.subtitle: The 25-card insert that puts Ray Allen in Supersonics green next to current stars
featuredVisualizations.caption: Cards YK-1 through YK-15 are current stars. YK-16 through YK-25 are HOF legends in their original/iconic team colors. Ray Allen's Seattle Supersonics card is the specific annotation — a team that hasn't existed since the 2008 OKC relocation. Topps deliberately chose this era.

players.annotation: Team defunct since 2008 — Topps chose the pre-OKC Sonics era.

featuredVisualizations.note: The Young Kings insert is the sleeper content of this product.

featuredVisualizations.title: Four Inserts. Same Players. Same Odds.
featuredVisualizations.subtitle: The structural redundancy Topps built into the NCAA side
featuredVisualizations.caption: Talent Tracker, Gen Next, Very Important Prospects, and Bowman Verified share the same 25-player NCAA pool and identical pack odds (1:58 Hobby across all four). Only the design differs. Talent Tracker is the flagship; the others are collector filler.

featuredVisualizations.title: The Mega-Only Mojo Ladder
featuredVisualizations.subtitle: 8 Mojo parallels. 0 Hobby. 0 Jumbo. 0 Value.
featuredVisualizations.caption: If you want Mojo, you buy Mega. The entire color ladder is locked behind one format — Blue Mojo /150 at the top down to Rose Gold Mojo /1 as the floor. This is the clearest format-driven incentive in the product.
featuredVisualizations.note: Mojo parallels exist on base cards, Chrome Prospects, auto cards, AND in Mega Rookies/Mega Prospects inserts — all Mega-only.

featuredVisualizations.title: Case-Hit Probability Spread
featuredVisualizations.subtitle: 11 case hits, very different accessibility
featuredVisualizations.caption: Not all case hits are equal. Anime and Spotlights are roughly 1:1,500-1:1,700 Hobby — tough but reachable. GPK NIL at 1:16,288 is effectively a per-multi-case hit. Bubble size inverse to odds: bigger = more accessible.

caseHits.name: Chrome Prospect Etched In Glass Variation

funTakeaways.headline: The Flagg brothers are both 1st Bowmans
funTakeaways.body: Cooper Flagg anchors the NBA base set at card #1 with Dallas. Younger brother Ace Flagg plays at the University of Maine and lands on BPP-91. Sibling set chase is real — and pairing a Cooper paper /1 with an Ace paper /1 is a specific collector goal.

funTakeaways.headline: Ray Allen's Young Kings card is in a Seattle Supersonics jersey
funTakeaways.body: The Supersonics franchise hasn't existed since 2008. Topps deliberately chose Ray Allen's pre-OKC era — before Durant, before the relocation — for his YK-25 legend card. It's the only card in the product referencing a defunct NBA franchise.

funTakeaways.headline: The missing NBA rookies all share one thing: likely Panini exclusives
funTakeaways.body: VJ Edgecombe (#3), Tre Johnson III (#6), and Jeremiah Fears (#7) appear in the base set, Chrome Variations, Rookie Reds, and insert sets — but zero autographs. No other pattern unifies them. The common factor is probably Panini-exclusive auto contracts signed before Topps got NBA rights.

funTakeaways.headline: Shaq appears in this product three different ways
funTakeaways.body: Young Kings YK-19: Shaq in Lakers yellow (modern star). Retrofractor RV-SA: Shaq at LSU in college purple-and-gold (throwback variation). Retrofractor Autograph RAV-SO: Shaq's LSU card, signed (1-per-case tier). Bill Russell shares the Retrofractor pair (San Francisco era) but not the auto since he's deceased.

funTakeaways.headline: Four insert sets have identical everything except the design
funTakeaways.body: Talent Tracker, Gen Next, Very Important Prospects, and Bowman Verified each have 25 cards, identical pack odds (1:58 Hobby), the same 25 NCAA players, and the same parallel rainbow structure. Only the wrapper design changes. Talent Tracker is the flagship; the others are collector filler dressed as premium content.
